group_1.narration: We couldn't find Yuu-san or Shii-san,@nor could we find John-san@or any of the other dead guys.@We felt like we were under a spell.
We were able to telepathically link up@with Nagisa-san to regroup with her, but...

group_1.textLeft: ...So basically...

group_1.textRight: Yuu-san AND Shii-san got away?!
W-well, uh, I guess so? That's about it!
Buuut... Did I make the blunder of a lifetime?@Probably!

group_1.textLeft: I wonder...what'll come of the incident Yuu-san@caused...

group_1.textRight: What Yuu and Shii did...
...has nothing to do with Magical Girls.
That's just a regular serial murder case.
Not something we should be sticking our noses@into.
They'll probably get canned for it eventually.

group_1.textLeft: (The two of them must've dumped the evidence@in a Witch's Labyrinth...)
(Which reminds me of some of the things@that Nagisa-san said about Hotsure.)
(Like Magical Girls openly causing trouble...)
(She seems to think that nobody's going to catch@onto those two, but...)
Yuu-san and Shii-san committed those crimes@for a reason.
What's the likelihood that they're going to keep@doing that...?

group_1.textRight: Eek... It's gonna be bad news if we don't stop@them...!
E-everything's gonna be just fine, okay?

group_1.textLeft: What makes you so sure...?

group_1.textRight: Uhh, um...
Actually, after that, Shii made a contract and@became a Magical Girl.
She wanted the power to, I dunno, reform bad@guys or something...
Yup, that was it.
It's more efficient to rehabilitate bad guys,@you see!
Wh-what...?

group_1.textLeft: ...Uh-huh.

group_1.textRight: Mmmgh...
(You think they're onto me...?)

group_1.textLeft: [chara:104701:effect_emotion_joy_0][se:7222_happy]Whew, that's a relief to hear!
Sounds like becoming a Magical Girl made her@turn over a new leaf!
Y...yeah. Sure. I bet it did...

group_1.textRight: I can tell you're nervous, too, Kuroe...
...but I'll handle the job of keeping an eye on@Yuu and Shii.
The two of you can carry on with your lives@as normal!

group_1.textLeft: Ah, but I don't think we can leave everything up@to you...

group_1.textRight: Nagisa here is 500 times stronger than you,@so don't sweat it.
If anything happens, I'll make sure to show 'em@what-for!

group_1.textLeft: Uh...huh. Well, if you say so...

group_1.textRight: You worry too much.
(In this universe...)
(Maybe living with a curse still counts as@freedom.)
...Kuroe.

group_1.textLeft: What?

group_1.textRight: Magical Girls have still gotta live for themselves@first.
Leave all the stuff about other people...
...to those who can afford the brain space@to think about them.

group_1.textLeft: ...Yeah.

group_1.textRight: I meeaaan...
As far as helping people goes...
...even something as simple as sharing cheese@with them counts, doesn't it?
All this "fate" and "mission" stuff is restrictive@and the opposite of free.

group_1.textLeft: Well, you're...not wrong? But what brings@this on?

group_1.textRight: ...Kuroe. You— just you— should keep on running.
Up until "tomorrow" ends...
...you've gotta bounce whenever a challenge@comes your way.

group_1.textLeft: I won't. But I'll be fine, alright?
I'm sure that Tamaki-san would tackle these@things head-on...
So I won't run away anymore, even if I...

group_1.narration: Those who should have returned remain.
The boundaries of hope and despair blur together,@and the fabric of the universe slowly wears thin.
The cause and effect from a Hotsure@cannot be restored.
But in spite of that, we continue to live our lives—@messily tangled together, haphazardly mending@the patches with the threads we've gathered...

group_1.textCenter: (People who can't compromise are a real pain@in the neck.)

group_1.textRight: Anyway, we better split here.
Let's get outta this dump already.
I'm gonna go have some cheese at Mami's place.

group_1.textLeft: Yeah... Goodbye.
See you again soon!

group_1.textRight: Well...
A whole ton of stuff happened...
...but there's no other choice than to keep@plugging away like I always have!

group_1.textLeft: Back to business as usual...
...Yeah, that's what Tamaki-san would've wanted,@too...

group_1.textRight: Ah! Of course, I'm not gonna forget about@my goals!
This all reminded me...
...of just how tiny and weak I am.
So I'm gonna do my best to hurry up and become@a full-fledged adult who can beat Witches!

group_1.textCenter: (Even if I feel miserable or pathetic...)
(I'll continue to live the way I always have...@As myself.)
(That's what girls like Tamaki-san taught me,@after all.)
(I still didn't get to say goodbye to her in@the end...)

group_1.narration: To be honest,@I'm still not sure I can handle the idea@of my memories of you@getting replaced by new stuff.
But I'll try to carry on anyway.
Past where things ended when you left,@and past the beginning of a world@without you in it.
When I can't remember your face or voice anymore...
I know it'll come back to me.
I'll lose you piece by piece,@but the world is going to be just fine.
One day,@when even Magical Girls can't tell the difference@between truth and fairytales...
I'm sure I'll think of you.
And I'll cry just one last time.
In an era where the rough peak@has been rounded off,@and become the mother@of the pebbles in the garden...
...and the sprouts that sprang up yesterday@have been transformed into a magnificent shrine...
"Huh... Isn't that the thing@about that terrifying runaway Doppel?"
"Excuse me?!@Yodaka-sama is an apostle from time immemorial@who's graced our poor souls@with the legend of the Magia Record,@which was born long ago..."
"...as well as the story of how@the Magical Girls of this world were saved—@and the story of a single girl."
"Nuh-uh, there's no way that's true.@Isn't it, like, some kinda bird monster?"
"No? She's an actual human being.@She even told me her name!"
"It's the same as the name@of the faith she's devoted to..."
The story will no longer continue.@This is the last page.
